blocks.content: Fleeing from the pursuit of the holy knights, Shirone's group paused to catch their breath in a secluded area of the Papal residence.
The area was lined with prayer rooms, empty and exuding an eerie, damp atmosphere.
Shirone spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I doubt they'd do anything in such a public space. Let's head underground.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But they'll likely be guarding it.
Neid countered.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: If they know we haven't left the Papal residence, they'll probably guess what we're planning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then we'll have no choice but to break through head-on...
A sobbing sound echoed down the hallway.
Hhh… Hhh…
Leaning against the wall, Shirone peeked around the corner to find a woman holding a spear, crying bitterly as she walked.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Oh, god Crea, why is the world so full of filth? Grant me the courage to make all bow before your feet…
Neid frowned.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What's wrong with her? Is she insane?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In a way.
Shirone replied knowingly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's likely one of the Inquisition's holy knights. If I'm right… she's one of the 'Weeping Ones.'

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The Weeping Ones?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's a title given to members of the Inquisition: the Weeping Ones, the Laughing Ones, the Pleading Ones, the Angry Ones. And the leader of the Inquisition's holy knights is known as the Forgiving One.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: That's extreme—restricting human emotions to a single type.
Neid remarked.
Shirone nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I heard it was abolished, but according to Omega's records, the holy knights of the Ramiel Church were trained from birth through extreme mental and physical discipline. The Inquisition's training was particularly…
Shirone hesitated.
He was unwilling to describe its horrors.
The woman's trembling voice drew nearer.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Oh god, punish me, a sinner.
Snapping out of his thoughts, Neid suggested,

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Let's take her down. Dragging someone like that around will only slow us down.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Don't overdo it this time.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: We don't need a repeat of earlier.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Yeah, yeah. I just misjudged my strength earlier.
Electricity sparked between his fingers.
Rounding the corner, he rushed toward the woman.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: One hit to knock her out.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Hhh… Oh god…
As Neid closed in with his spark magic, he struck her neck with a jolt of electricity.
The woman's body convulsed violently.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Got her.
But just as he thought so, her body twisted unnaturally, and the tip of her spear shot toward his throat with terrifying speed.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Guh!
Neid bent backward in midair, narrowly avoiding the blade.
The woman screamed.
The scream sounded like a prayer.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Oh god!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's fast.
Even Shirone would struggle to catch Neid in his Thunder God Reincarnation state, but the woman's movements were almost supernatural.
Dodging the spear, Neid redirected himself with a crackle of static and charged his palm with electricity.
Crackle of static

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Take this!
Impact
The sound of impact was followed by the smell of scorched fabric as the woman dropped her spear and fell to the ground, trembling uncontrollably.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now's my chance.
Shirone darted forward, pressing the light of Agape to her forehead as he had done before.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Ah… Ah…
The woman's tears seemed to pause momentarily—
—but blood soon trickled from beneath her eyes.
Barely able to move her lips, her throat rasped out harsh words.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: You filthy wretch.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The purification didn't work. The brainwashing runs too deep.

blocks.speaker: Woman
blocks.content: Die!
The woman lunged with her claws raised, but Neid intercepted her, delivering a dual shock to her back.
Bang
Her body flew over Shirone's head and slammed into the hallway wall.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hah… Hah…
Neid panted heavily.
Even Iruki refrained from scolding him this time.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: She's a fanatic. That was close.
Iruki approached the fallen woman and checked her pulse.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: She's alive, but… look at her back.
Where her scorched clothing revealed her skin, scars of horrific torture were visible.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Was she whipped? How does a body even endure this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Self-inflicted.
Shirone said, stepping closer.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Inquisition's role is to root out heretics through interrogation and torture. But the Ramiel Church decreed that they must first inflict equal suffering upon themselves before doing so to others.
The group grew somber as Shirone continued.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Otherwise, they can't draw upon their divine powers. But enduring such agony leaves scars—not just physical but emotional—that can be projected onto others without any guilt.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I see.
Iruki murmured.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: They pass on the suffering without hesitation.
Shirone nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's troubling. If Agape can't purify her, then…
Laughter echoed from the opposite hallway.

blocks.speaker: Laughing One
blocks.content: Heeheehee. Oh god, I'm so happy.
As shadowy figures appeared, the group instinctively turned and fled around the corner.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Creepy. What now? If purification doesn't work, do we just beat them all down?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Catching them one by one won't end this. We need evidence. Let's find the path to the underground…
Shirone suddenly stopped in his tracks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Damn it.
From the other end of the hallway, ten holy knights approached, their hands clasped together in prayer.

blocks.speaker: Pleading Ones
blocks.content: Oh god, bless this world. We pray for all to be happy.
The Pleading Ones.
Their murmured prayers filled the air, while laughter grew louder from behind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We'll have to fight, won't we?
At that moment, a familiar face appeared as a door to a prayer room opened.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: This way.
It was Seina.
Trusting her was better than facing the holy knights.
The group quickly slipped into the room.
Seina activated a mechanism in the fireplace, revealing a hidden passage.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Follow me. Quickly. If they catch us, I'm done for too.
As they entered the secret passage, Seina suddenly grabbed Shirone by the collar.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: You! Yahweh!
Neid and Iruki rushed to intervene, but Shirone raised a hand to stop them.
Seina's eyes burned with fury.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: What did you do to me? Why is my divine power weakening?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nothing. The brainwashing has been undone.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Brainwashing?
Her grip loosened momentarily, but then she hoisted Shirone up with even greater force.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: What nonsense is this? I haven't been brainwashed—I'm a Paladin of the Oracle Bureau!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You don't need me to tell you; deep down, you already know.
After a pause, Shirone's feet returned to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Ugh, this is driving me crazy.
Shirone continued,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's something I want to ask. The harsh training of the holy knights—is it still ongoing?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: …Officially, it's been abolished.
Seina admitted reluctantly.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: But since the Great War, there's been a movement to revive it. The number of holy knights has dropped far below pre-war levels.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What about the Inquisition?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: I don't know much about them. Even among the holy knights, only those ranked up to third in the Papal hierarchy have access to the Inquisition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The third rank? The first is the Pope, the second?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: …The Inquisition's Paladin Commander, Vanisa.
Seina replied.
The Forgiving One.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the second rank is the source of the problem, that leaves only the third.
Shirone reasoned.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: That's right, High Priest Maximus.
Seina confirmed.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: But he's unwell and in seclusion right now. He was injured when a woman named Lucia awakened her demonic powers.
Iruki asked,

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What kind of training do the holy knights undergo?

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Anything to oppose evil. They're pushed to their mental and physical limits while reciting verses like, 'We serve God loyally,' or 'We reject the words of heretics.'
Seina's body trembled slightly as she spoke.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: It's grueling, but at the time, I didn't think it was unfair. It felt like a necessary process to fight evil. But now… I'm not so sure.
She began to doubt whether it had been her own will.
Seina turned to Shirone, her eyes blazing.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Even if I received your blessing, my faith in Crea hasn't wavered! So why is my divine power weakening?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You can recover it.
Shirone said gently.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Once the doubt in your heart is cleared. You need to rediscover your faith on your own. If you're uncertain, confirm it now. Whatever you choose, make sure it's your decision.
Seina bit her lip, lost in thought, before finally asking,

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: What should I do?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Take us to the basement of the Papal residence. Something is happening there. Using forceful methods will never lead to true goodness.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: …The basement belongs to the Inquisition. It's deep underground, so even within the Papal residence, we rarely encounter it.
Seina said hesitantly.
Resolving herself, she turned to face them.

blocks.speaker: Seina
blocks.content: Follow me. I don't know how much help I'll be, but I'll do what I can.

blocks.speaker: Announcer
blocks.content: The Kingdom of Paras! Entering!
While other nations rested within the main hall of the Papal residence, the Kingdom of Paras arrived.
That was quick.
Even with teleportation magic, such speed was impossible without top-tier mobility.
At the forefront of the parade, a man lounged in a golden carriage, a massive serpent coiled around his neck.
Kitra. The new King of Paras.
Though the Middle East was currently dominated by Kashan, everything Kashan had originated in Paras.
The cradle of civilization.
The procession exuded pride in being the birthplace of intellect.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: They seem formidable.
Lupist murmured.
Shirone remained silent.
He was deep in thought.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: What's on your mind?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's nothing.
Shirone replied quickly.
In truth, he was locked in a confrontation within the Truth Pyramid with the entity known as Human.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Emotion sickness.
Shirone muttered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's possible that King Kitra has been cured of it.
The Paras procession reached the front of the Papal residence.
Kitra met Shirone's gaze through the window and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Kitra
blocks.content: Have you received the baptism of Human?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I knew it.
Shirone was certain.
The reason King Kitra approved the exploration of the Truth Pyramid...
Kitra's piercing gaze sparkled ominously.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He's already been inside.
Kitra smirked coldly, as if he could read Shirone's thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Kitra
blocks.content: The burden of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Kitra
blocks.content: Cause and effect.

blocks.speaker: Kitra
blocks.content: It's truly ironic.
A robed man approached the king and whispered,

blocks.speaker: Robed Man
blocks.content: Your Majesty, the time has come to alter fate.
Shirone did not miss the glint of madness that swept through Kitra's eyes.
Astrologers. The most dangerous of all.
The Zodiac Council of Paras was their national think tank, and they were not to be underestimated.
Within the twisted space of the Truth Pyramid, Lucia wandered, searching frantically.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Glenn! Where are you, Glenn?

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Please be safe.
Her powers were effective in subduing foes, but Glenn specialized only in healing.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Where did you go?
Taking another step forward, she suddenly found herself in a different scene.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Where is this?
It was the seminary under the Papal residence—the place where Lucia had first met Glenn.
In the flashback, a teenage Glenn and Lucia ran about an empty chapel as if it were a playground.
Lucia smiled faintly at the memory.

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: How carefree those days were—when simply playing with a friend was enough.
But their laughter was cut short as a nun stormed in.

blocks.speaker: Nun
blocks.content: What are you doing in this sacred place? Today is an important day!

blocks.speaker: Glenn & Lucia
blocks.content: Ah!
Both froze in fear as an older man entered behind the nun.

blocks.speaker: Older Man
blocks.content: Now, now, don't be too harsh.
He said with a chuckle.

blocks.speaker: Older Man
blocks.content: They're just kids. I was the same at their age.
It was High Priest Maximus, the current third in rank at the Papal residence.
